title: The Maxims of Ptahhotep
description: The oldest complete book in the world: a vizier's instructions on justice, humility, and the art of listening.
introduction: Written around 2350 BCE, the Maxims of Ptahhotep may be the oldest complete book in human history. The aged vizier Ptahhotep instructs his son in the arts of governance, persuasion, and just dealing. The text's emphasis on listening over speaking, humility over pride, and justice as the foundation of social order places it in a wisdom tradition that influenced Proverbs, Ecclesiastes, and Greek gnomology.

translation.original_text: jw ḥmw.t r wn m-ḫnt s.t-jb
translation.text: Wisdom is rarer than emeralds, yet it can be found among the women at the grindstones.

translation.original_text: m jri gr.t ḥr wḥm n mdw
translation.text: Do not be proud because of your knowledge. Consult the ignorant as well as the wise.
